pattern: Recording each target value's depth and immediate parent during one traversal makes the cousin test a comparison of equal depth with differing parents.
hints: Two nodes are cousins exactly when they sit at the same depth but hang from different parents.
So you only need each target's depth and the value of its parent.
Traverse tracking depth and parent; capture (depth, parent) for x and for y, then return true when their depths match and their parents differ.

edge_inputs.description: siblings share a parent so not cousins
same depth different parents are cousins
same parent at deeper level not cousins

stress.description: random unique-valued tree with two distinct present targets
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, min(size, 100))
    vals = rng.sample(range(1, 101), n)
    arr = [vals[0]]
    idx = 1
    # build a random shaped tree level-order, sometimes inserting holes
    while idx < n:
        if rng.random() < 0.85:
            arr.append(vals[idx])
            idx += 1
        else:
            arr.append(None)
    # ensure at least two real values present
    present = [v for v in arr if v is not None]
    x, y = rng.sample(present, 2)
    return [arr, x, y]
stress.note: unique values; x and y are two distinct values that exist in the tree
